featured_cases.title: Embedding data into design decision-making
featured_cases.framing:
BBVA had multiple internal data sources and analytical tools available to design teams, but availability alone was not translating into practice. Knowledge was uneven, access was partial, and the use of data in everyday design work lacked a shared structure.

The challenge was not simply to expose teams to more analytics, but to turn data into something usable inside the design process — so that research, hypotheses, prioritization, and evaluation could be grounded in evidence rather than convenience or intuition.
featured_cases.shift:
From — Data available but weakly embedded in practice
To — A structured, measurable, data-informed design practice
featured_cases.contribution:
Co-designed and implemented the program to institutionalize data-informed design across the organization.

My work focused on translating the internal data ecosystem into usable design logic, structuring frameworks and rituals around it, and helping embed data into decision-making, team habits, and project workflows.
key_moves_and_learnings:
— Mapped the internal data ecosystem and its points of friction
— Clarified what was available, what was unknown, and where access or literacy gaps were blocking use

— Turned data sources into design tools rather than technical abstractions
— Helped designers understand when and how to use different inputs inside their process

— Structured the practice through playbooks, rituals, and enablement formats
— Embedded data into recurring workflows instead of treating it as optional support

— Built reinforcement mechanisms around learning and adoption
— Combined capability building with practical examples, communication, and continuous follow-up

— Measured the evolution of the practice over time
— Used KPIs to understand where adoption was growing and where structural barriers still remained
outcome: Data tagging with a design perspective reached 79% of projects
Consumption of accumulated Adobe Analytics information reached 67% of projects
Knowledge of the three most popular data tools reached 84–87% of the team
The discipline gained a clearer, more consistent way of incorporating data into design decisions

featured_cases.title: Turning Global Strategy into an Executable Program
featured_cases.framing:
BBVA's global positioning introduced a new strategic narrative centered on a radical customer perspective.

However, the strategy was highly conceptual, leaving room for multiple interpretations and making it difficult to translate into concrete product, service, and experience decisions — especially in parallel with a design system relaunch and ongoing product delivery cycles.
featured_cases.shift:
From — Abstract strategic narrative open to interpretation
To — A structured program aligned with product delivery and team execution
featured_cases.contribution:
Structured and facilitated the translation of global strategy into an executable program.

Focused on connecting the strategic narrative to concrete ways of building products, services, and experiences, aligning design, technology, and business teams around a shared direction that could operate within existing delivery rhythms.
key_moves_and_learnings:
— Identified abstraction as the core barrier to execution
— The strategy aligned at a narrative level, but not at a decision-making level

— Translated the positioning into actionable lenses for product and service design
— Helped teams understand how the strategy should shape what they build

— Structured the work to align with product release rhythms
— Enabled integration into ongoing delivery instead of creating parallel tracks

— Facilitated alignment across design, technology, and business
— Reduced divergent interpretations and created shared direction

— Framed execution as a program, not a one-off translation
— Enabled continuous delivery rather than static strategy
outcome: Clearer connection between global strategy and day-to-day product and service decisions
Reduced ambiguity in how teams interpret and apply the strategy
Increased coherence in company output, with product and service teams delivering consistently aligned efforts toward the new positioning

featured_cases.title: Turning a design discipline into a measurable and evolving system
featured_cases.framing:
As BBVA's design discipline grew in scale and strategic importance, it lacked a unified system to consistently track its contribution to business objectives, align priorities across teams, and guide its evolution over time.

Multiple initiatives were in motion, but there was no clear way to connect strategic priorities, team activities, deliverables, and their contribution to global OKRs into a coherent operational view.
featured_cases.shift:
From — Distributed initiatives with limited visibility and alignment
To — A structured system connecting strategy, execution, and performance
featured_cases.contribution:
Owned the design and structuring of the discipline's operational system and its yearly reporting model.

Defined how strategic priorities translate into activities, deliverables, and measurable outcomes, and built the report as a tracking mechanism to make this system visible — connecting strategy, execution, and contribution to business objectives.
key_moves_and_learnings:
— Structured the discipline around clear strategic pillars
— Organized all initiatives under a shared framework connecting customer impact, operational excellence, and team evolution

— Defined the logic linking strategy to execution
— Connected priorities, activities, deliverables, and OKRs into a single system

— Designed the yearly planning and backlog construction process
— Enabled teams to review, prioritize, and define initiatives aligned with the next strategic cycle

— Built a measurable tracking system
— Made visible progress across 29 activities and 54 deliverables, including contribution to global OKRs

— Integrated cross-functional collaboration into the model
— Connected design with insights, strategy, and business teams through shared planning and execution

— Positioned the report as an operational tool, not just communication
— Enabled continuous alignment, decision-making, and strategic evolution
outcome: Clear visibility of design's contribution to global strategy and OKRs
Improved alignment across teams, initiatives, and deliverables
A structured backlog and planning system adopted across the discipline
Increased ability to evolve strategy year over year based on measurable progress
Stronger positioning of design as a strategic and operational driver within the organization

featured_cases.title: Transforming core banking products into a coherent system
featured_cases.framing:
BBVA's core products — accounts and cards — functioned as isolated solutions, shaped by a bank-centric logic and fragmented across channels and lifecycle moments.

While they supported key financial operations, they lacked a unifying structure that could connect product features, experiences, and services into a coherent system aligned with customer needs and strategic intent.
featured_cases.shift:
From — Fragmented, feature-driven products
To — A unified product system grounded in financial health
featured_cases.contribution:
As part of the core design team driving the initiative, I contributed to reframing BBVA's core products into a unified system.

My focus was on defining the product architecture and translating strategic principles into operational logic — connecting products, features, services, channels, and lifecycle experiences into a structure that could guide product decisions and prioritization.
key_moves_and_learnings:
— Reframed products around financial health as a unifying principle
— Shifted the logic from product-centric to outcome-driven design

— Structured a product architecture model
— Defined relationships between accounts, cards, services, and features

— Connected product definition with lifecycle and channel experiences
— Ensured consistency across digital and assisted touchpoints

— Translated strategic intent into operational frameworks
— Enabled teams to align on priorities and structure product evolution

— Worked across product, design, business, and technology
— Facilitated shared understanding in a highly cross-functional environment
outcome: Clearer structure for how core products operate as a system
Improved alignment in product decision-making and prioritization
Increased coherence across product features, services, and experiences
Foundation for scaling a shared product methodology across teams

featured_cases.title: Building a foresight capability beyond exploration into execution
featured_cases.framing:
Interest in strategic foresight was growing both internally and from clients, but the capability was still emerging and unevenly developed.

There was no shared practice that allowed teams to respond consistently to demand, nor a clear way to connect foresight work to concrete business, product, or technology outcomes.
featured_cases.shift:
From — Foresight as exploratory research and isolated initiatives
To — A shared capability connecting future scenarios to actionable strategies and solutions
featured_cases.contribution:
Led the development and scaling of a strategic foresight capability within the organization.

Focused on building a shared practice that combined methodology, enablement, and commercial application — connecting foresight to business strategy, design, and technology to move from exploration into execution.
key_moves_and_learnings:
— Positioned foresight beyond trend analysis into strategic and operational impact
— Expanded its role from insight generation to shaping products, services, and solutions

— Built a shared methodology and toolkit
— Enabled teams to apply foresight consistently across projects

— Developed internal capability through training, templates, and rituals
— Allowed the practice to scale beyond a single team

— Connected foresight to commercial offerings and business development
— Turned capability into a source of new opportunities

— Established thought leadership through reports, content, and events
— Increased visibility and credibility internally and externally

— Leveraged cross-functional collaboration (strategy, technology, design)
— Enabled translation of future scenarios into tangible solutions
outcome: Teams began incorporating foresight methodologies into projects where relevant
Increased internal credibility and recognition of the practice
Expanded use of foresight in sales proposals and positioning
Stronger engagement with senior stakeholders, including C-level conversations
Generated new business opportunities through foresight-led work

featured_cases.title: Shaping a new insurance product from an uncertain market opportunity
featured_cases.framing:
Nationale Nederlanden identified an emerging opportunity to develop a protection product for seniors, but the need was still diffuse and not clearly defined.

While initial research provided directional insights, it was not sufficient to confidently define a product. The challenge was to build evidence around a scattered market need, and to use a concrete concept as a way to explore, test, and understand what a relevant product could be in today's context.
featured_cases.shift:
From — A diffuse opportunity with limited evidence
To — A validated product concept grounded in real customer needs
featured_cases.contribution:
Facilitated the definition and validation of a new insurance product as part of a team of service and strategic designers.

Led workshops to define product features and shape the value proposition, and structured the validation process through qualitative research and interviews — using the concept as a tool to generate evidence, test assumptions, and refine the product.
key_moves_and_learnings:
— Framed the concept as a tool for exploration and validation
— Used the product itself to generate evidence, not just to deliver a solution

— Facilitated workshops to define product features and value proposition
— Aligned the team around a structured offer under uncertainty

— Structured and executed qualitative validation
— Designed interviews to test understanding, relevance, and decision drivers

— Translated insights into product and service logic
— Organized coverages, services, and modalities into a coherent system

— Tangibilized the concept into testable assets
— Developed landing, product definition, and journey to enable validation

— Identified real value drivers beyond initial assumptions
— Services such as caregiver support and family-oriented features proved more relevant than traditional coverage
outcome: A validated product concept grounded in real customer needs and decision drivers
Clearer understanding of the opportunity space and how to approach it
A structured value proposition and product logic aligned with market expectations
Tangible assets enabling internal alignment, validation, and future development

featured_cases.title: Designing the Infrastructure for Craft Innovation in Peru
featured_cases.framing:
Peru has one of the most culturally rich craft traditions, yet the ecosystem operates in a fragmented way — with disconnected initiatives, limited coordination between institutions, and weak structures to support long-term economic development.

The project originated from a private stakeholder's intent to invest in the Ayacucho region, aiming to preserve craft heritage while generating economic opportunity. What began as the idea of a craft innovation center quickly revealed a broader challenge: not the lack of initiatives, but the absence of a system capable of connecting education, production, and market access.
featured_cases.shift:
From — Fragmented initiatives with limited coordination
To — A structured infrastructure connecting craft, education, and economic development
featured_cases.contribution:
Led the end-to-end development of the project — from defining the opportunity and shaping the offer, to designing the methodology, conducting research, and structuring the full system.

Directed both the research and design work, defining the craft innovation center, its educational model, and the ecosystem programs required to connect stakeholders across public institutions, private investment, and local craft communities.
key_moves_and_learnings:
— Defined the opportunity and structured the initial offer
— Positioned the project as an investment in both cultural preservation and economic development

— Developed the full research and design methodology
— Conducted ecosystem analysis, stakeholder interviews, and synthesis to identify structural gaps

— Reframed the challenge from a single initiative to a system-level problem
— Shifted focus from building a center to designing an infrastructure

— Designed the craft innovation center as a system node
— Integrated research, education, innovation, and commercialization

— Defined the educational model and syllabus
— Connected traditional craft knowledge with contemporary design and business practices

— Structured programs to strengthen the broader ecosystem
— Focused on SMEs supporting artisans, aligned with real operational constraints

— Led stakeholder alignment across public and private actors
— Connected local institutions, international organizations, and private stakeholders

— Learned that impact depends on articulation, not activity
— Value comes from connecting existing efforts, not multiplying initiatives
outcome: A fully defined model for a craft innovation infrastructure, including center, programs, and ecosystem logic
Project roadmap ready for implementation, covering campus, syllabus, and operational structure
Secured interest and funding from international institutions, including Spanish Cooperation and the Inter-American Development Bank
One initiative awarded funding and activated as a program supporting SMEs within the craft ecosystem
Multiple yearly cycles executed, strengthening the network of professionals supporting artisan businesses
A scalable approach to connecting craft, education, and economic development

level_2_projects.title: Designing an Omnichannel Self-Service Ecosystem
level_2_projects.core_tension: Customers were defaulting to assisted channels because self-service journeys did not work coherently across touchpoints.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Fragmented self-service attempts
To — A coordinated self-service ecosystem
level_2_projects.contribution: Structured the target self-service logic across channels, aligning customer flows, service principles, and operational responsibilities.
level_2_projects.outcome: A clearer omnichannel service model aimed at reducing unnecessary calls and improving autonomous resolution.

level_2_projects.title: Designing an NPS Intelligence Framework for High-Value Routes
level_2_projects.core_tension: The organization lacked a structured understanding of what was driving satisfaction and dissatisfaction on its most strategically important routes.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Broad NPS readings
To — Route-level experience drivers
level_2_projects.contribution: Built a mixed-methods framework combining interviews, benchmark analysis, and route-level data to identify actionable performance levers.
level_2_projects.outcome: Enabled more precise prioritization of experience improvements on high-value routes.

level_2_projects.title: Defining the Strategic Vision for the Integrated Operations Control Center
level_2_projects.core_tension: Iberia needed a coherent vision for an operations control center integrating human teams, crisis logic, and AI.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Space-led thinking
To — Integrated operational system design
level_2_projects.contribution: Defined the systemic and spatial logic of the future iOCC through workflow mapping, scenario modeling, and organizational design.
level_2_projects.outcome: A strategic blueprint aligning teams around a future-ready model for coordination and decision-making.

level_2_projects.title: Conceptual and Architectural Strategy for a Vacation Home
level_2_projects.core_tension: The client needed a coherent concept and business rationale grounded in site conditions and material logic.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Intuitive brief
To — Environment-led architectural concept
level_2_projects.contribution: Led environmental, geospatial, and material research to define the concept, architectural direction, and business case.
level_2_projects.outcome: A concept-level strategy ready to guide the next phase of development.

level_2_projects.title: Redefining the Young Segment Value Proposition
level_2_projects.core_tension: BBVA's young-segment offer was not resonating and lacked a differentiated proposition grounded in real behaviors and aspirations.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Generic segment offer
To — Research-based product direction
level_2_projects.contribution: Co-led mixed-methods research and translated findings into persona logic, value proposition architecture, and MVP prioritization.
level_2_projects.outcome: A clearer strategic direction for the segment and a more grounded basis for product prioritization.

level_2_projects.title: Bachelor Thesis Advisory Program
level_2_projects.core_tension: Students needed structured methodological support to turn research and concepts into rigorous thesis work.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Dispersed academic effort
To — Structured thesis development
level_2_projects.contribution: Guided students through research design, critique loops, and evaluation standards across their thesis process.
level_2_projects.outcome: Supported stronger, more defensible final thesis outcomes.

level_2_projects.title: SME Strategic Pop-Up Accelerator
level_2_projects.core_tension: Small businesses lacked structured ways to diagnose their position and define growth actions quickly.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Informal business decisions
To — Structured acceleration support
level_2_projects.contribution: Designed an accelerator format to help SMEs translate challenges into clearer priorities and business actions.
level_2_projects.outcome: A practical support model for improving strategic clarity in small businesses.

level_2_projects.title: Designing an Internal Innovation Toolkit
level_2_projects.core_tension: Innovation efforts lacked a shared toolkit to support teams in exploring and structuring opportunities consistently.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Ad hoc innovation practice
To — Shared innovation toolkit
level_2_projects.contribution: Designed tools and guidance to help internal teams frame, explore, and develop innovation initiatives.
level_2_projects.outcome: A more structured way for teams to approach internal innovation work.

level_2_projects.title: Leading the Strategic and Brand Reset
level_2_projects.core_tension: The brand needed a clearer strategic direction and renewed positioning.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Diffuse brand direction
To — Sharper strategic identity
level_2_projects.contribution: Led the strategic reframing of the brand and aligned its expression around a renewed direction.
level_2_projects.outcome: A clearer basis for brand evolution and communication.

level_2_projects.title: Establishing the Strategic Design Practice and Research Center
level_2_projects.core_tension: IED Madrid lacked a structured strategic design offer and the research and educational infrastructure to support it.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Emerging capability without operational or research foundation
To — A structured practice with a defined offer, methodology, and research center
level_2_projects.contribution: Built the practice model, methodologies, and positioning needed to establish and grow strategic design within the institution. Simultaneously defined the research center's logic, programs, and development direction across research and education activities.
level_2_projects.outcome: A clearer internal and external definition of the practice, its offer, and a stronger foundation for connecting research, learning, and external partnerships.

level_2_projects.title: Creative Industries Growth Network Program
level_2_projects.core_tension: Creative-industry actors needed stronger structures for exchange, growth, and collaboration.

From — Isolated actors

level_2_projects.contribution: Designed a program to connect participants around shared learning, collaboration, and growth opportunities.

level_2_projects.title: Impact Startup Mentorship Program
level_2_projects.core_tension: Early-stage impact startups needed sharper guidance to structure opportunities and decisions.

level_2_projects.contribution: Mentored founders through strategic, design, and business questions to help sharpen their proposals.

level_2_projects.outcome: A clearer product direction for a retail-oriented furniture line.

level_2_projects.core_tension: The space required a clearer strategic and experiential redesign to support how it should be used.

level_2_projects.contribution: Helped rethink the space and its experience logic to better align environment, use, and brand.
level_2_projects.outcome: A more coherent direction for the redesign of the site.

level_2_projects.core_tension: The country lacked a stronger connective structure for design and innovation actors to collaborate and scale impact.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Disconnected initiatives
To — National network logic
level_2_projects.contribution: Helped define a network model to connect institutions, opportunities, and actors around design and innovation.

level_2_projects.title: Masters in Design & Innovation Value Proposition
level_2_projects.core_tension: The program needed a sharper value proposition to clarify why it mattered and for whom.
level_2_projects.shift:
From — Generic academic positioning
